record_id_from_source_name.description: Extract record id from the report/source filename. It will look like TCELL or TCLA or LYMREG with a number before or after that pattern or just numbers like 12-123 with a number before or after that pattern.

not_report.description: Set to 'not_report' if the document is not a pathology report. Set to 'flow_citometry' if the document is flow cytometry. Otherwise use null.

patient_name.description: Full patient name exactly as written in report.

report_date.description: Date of pathology report. Prefer YYYY-MM-DD when possible.

ki67_percent.description: Ki-67 proliferation index as a numeric percent if present.

text_from_report.description: Extract all microscopy and immunohistochemistry text from the report and output it in English. The report may be in any language; translate it into English.

extraction_confidence.description: Overall confidence that the coded marker results are correct and complete: 'high' = text clear and results explicit; 'medium' = some ambiguity; 'low' = text garbled/OCR-poor or results unclear. Judge legibility and result clarity, not the diagnosis.

extraction_confidence_reason.description: One short sentence on what made the markers easy or hard to determine (e.g. clear text, OCR garbling, ambiguous or missing results).

Grade_follicular_lymphoma.description: 1 is Grade 1/I, 2 is Grade 2/II, 3 is Grade 3A/IIIA, 4 is Grade 3B/IIIB, only for follicular lymphoma

additional_markers.description: Catch-all for ANY immunohistochemistry or flow-cytometry marker reported that does NOT already have its own dedicated field above. Add one entry per such marker. Do NOT include markers that already have a dedicated field. Use null or an empty array if there are none.

marker.description: Standardized marker/antigen name in English (the antigen the antibody detects), e.g. CD20, PAX5, Ki-67, Cyclin D1, TdT, c-Myc, BCL2, MUM1. Do NOT use the vendor, catalog code, or antibody clone as the name (e.g. DAKO, polyclonal, clone L26 / Ber-H2 / MIB-1 / EP12 / EP266). If only a clone/vendor code is given and the antigen is NOT named, do not guess a CD/antigen number — a clone code is not a CD number (clone 124 is not CD124); keep the verbatim clone text as the marker name. Only convert textbook-certain clones (L26 -> CD20, MIB-1 -> Ki-67, Ber-H2 -> CD30), and prefer antigen names stated in the microscopy narrative. Translate non-English names to standard nomenclature (e.g. Кі-67 -> Ki-67, Циклін D1 -> Cyclin D1). Put the original written text (vendor / clone / percentage) in raw_text.

value.description: Same coding as the dedicated markers: 1 is positive (позитивний), 0 is negative (негативний), 2 is not done (не проводилось).

raw_text.description: The original result text for this marker as written in the report, if any (e.g. 'positive in 30% of cells').

required: ALK or CD246 or ALK protein
Immunoglobulins (IgM, IgD, IgG, IgA, kappa light chains, lambda light chains)
